title: Ann Marie's Sugar Cookies
author: Hannah Gunderman
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bbepijdn

steps.title: This is a holiday favorite to...
steps.instruction: This is a holiday favorite to cut into shapes for Christmas, Easter, Halloween and other special occasions. Adorn them with colored sugar sprinkles, dried fruit bits or tinted frosting. Why not try some pink frosting for Valentine's Day? Makes: about 4 dozen

steps.title: Ingredients 1/2 cup butter, room temperature...
steps.instruction: Ingredients 1/2 cup butter, room temperature 1/2 cup margarine, room temperature 1 1/4 cups sugar 2 eggs 1 tsp. vanilla extract 1/4 tsp. almond extract 3 1/2 cups all-purpose flour 1 tsp. baking powder 1/4 tsp. salt

steps.title: Beat butter and margarine until creamy
steps.instruction: Beat butter and margarine until creamy. Beat in sugar, eggs, vanilla and almond extract. In a separate bowl, stir together flour, baking powder and salt. Add to creamed mixture and beat well.

steps.title: Bake in a 350° oven for...
steps.instruction: Bake in a 350° oven for 8 to 10 minutes, or until lightly browned. Remove immediately to wire racks and cool.

steps.title: Share with a special someone
steps.instruction: Share with a special someone! This includes sharing them with yourself, because you are special!

steps.title: Cover and chill at least 1 hour
steps.instruction: Cover and chill at least 1 hour. Roll dough to l/8-inch thickness on a lightly floured board. Cut into desired shapes. Place on greased baking sheets and sprinkle with colored sugar if desired.
